content:
Key Findings

  • West Lancashire Borough Council spent £109.4M on Housing Revenue Account, accounting for 32.9% of total spending
  • Coronavirus business grants totalled £21.0M, representing 6.3% of the council's expenditure
  • Environmental Services received £18.0M, making up 5.4% of the total spending
  • Housing Public Sector spending amounted to £16.8M, or 5.1% of the total
  • Corporate and Customer Services were allocated £15.3M, which is 4.6% of the total expenditure

Housing Dominates Council Spending

West Lancashire Borough Council's largest expenditure category is the Housing Revenue Account, with a substantial £109.4M spent over the analysed period. This figure represents 32.9% of the total spending, highlighting the council's significant investment in housing services. The Housing Public Sector also received a notable £16.8M, further emphasising the priority placed on housing-related services.

The focus on housing is evident when compared to other departments. For instance, Environmental Services, which received £18.0M, accounts for 5.4% of the total spending. This disparity suggests that housing services are a key area of focus for the council, potentially reflecting the local demand for affordable and public housing solutions.

Coronavirus Business Grants: A Significant One-Off Expenditure

One of the most notable expenditures is the £21.0M allocated to Coronavirus business grants. This amount, which constitutes 6.3% of the total spending, underscores the council's response to the economic impact of the pandemic. The grants were likely crucial in supporting local businesses during a challenging period, although they represent a one-off expenditure that may not be repeated in future budgets.

Comparing this to other service items, the £20.3M spent on Other Service Items and the £11.6M on Non Service Items shows that while the coronavirus grants were significant, they were not the only areas of substantial spending. The council's budget allocation reflects a balance between immediate crisis response and ongoing service provision.

Departmental Spending Breakdown

The council's spending is distributed across various departments, each with its own set of responsibilities and financial requirements. The Housing Revenue Account's £109.4M expenditure is a clear outlier, but other departments also received significant allocations. For example, Corporate and Customer Services were allocated £15.3M, while Corporate Services received £13.7M. These figures highlight the council's commitment to maintaining essential services and administrative functions.

Housing and Inclusion, with £12.6M spent, also plays a crucial role in the council's service provision. This department's expenditure, though smaller than that of the Housing Revenue Account, is still substantial and indicates a focus on inclusion and support services within the community.

Department Amount % of Total
Housing Revenue Account £109.4M 32.9%
Coronavirus business grants £21.0M 6.3%
Other Service Items £20.3M 6.1%
Environmental Services £18.0M 5.4%
Housing Public Sector £16.8M 5.1%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to West Lancashire Borough Council about the specific allocations within the Housing Revenue Account
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about the budgeting process and how spending decisions are made
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see detailed information on contracts awarded for housing and environmental services
  • Write to your local councillor about the allocation of funds to the Housing Revenue Account and other key departments
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a deeper understanding of the council's financial priorities
This article is based on West Lancashire Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
